What we offer

Clinical care,
at your doorstep

Every service is delivered by a licensed registered nurse, personalised to your specific health needs and care goals.

Post-Surgical Nursing
Expert recovery care after surgery: wound management, IV therapy, pain assessment, and complication monitoring from a qualified OR-trained nurse.
Paediatric Home Care
Specialist nursing for infants, children, and teenagers: medication, growth monitoring, immunisation support, and parental guidance.
Chronic Disease Management
Ongoing monitoring for diabetes, hypertension, cancer, stroke, dementia, HIV/AIDS, Parkinson's, and other long-term conditions.
Post-Natal Care
Compassionate care for new mothers and newborns: lactation support, newborn assessment, maternal recovery, and family education.
Telehealth Consultations
Remote triage, virtual care coordination, and digital monitoring, ideal for diaspora families managing care of loved ones in Kenya.
Wound Care & IV Therapy
Professional wound dressing, IV cannulation, infusion therapy, and blood product administration, with clinical precision at home.
Elderly Home Care
Dignified, patient-centred care for the elderly: personal care assistance, mobility support, medication management and companionship.
Caregiver Training
Professional training for family members and hired caregivers, equipping them with skills to support patients safely at home.
Why choose Zuri

The difference you'll
feel from day one

We don't just send a nurse, we build a care partnership rooted in clinical excellence and genuine compassion.

01
International clinical standard
Our founder brings experience from a Military Medical City Hospital in Qatar, bringing world-class nursing protocols directly to your home.
02
Free initial home assessment
Before any care begins, we visit your home, meet the patient, and develop a fully customised care plan, at no charge.
03
Paediatric & surgical specialist
Rare dual expertise in paediatric nursing and OR/PACU care, meaning complex post-surgical and child health cases are handled with real confidence.
04
Telehealth for diaspora families
Are you abroad, caring for parents back home? We offer remote consultations, regular updates, and digital monitoring so you stay fully in the loop.
05
NCK licensed & professional
Fully registered with the Nursing Council of Kenya. Every service meets the highest professional and ethical standards.
06
Serving Nairobi & Kiambu
Based in Ruiru, Kiambu County, covering Nairobi, Kiambu, Thika, and surrounding areas with prompt, reliable home visits.
Meet the founder

A nurse who's
been there

Our founder brings over five years of clinical experience across Kenya and Qatar, from paediatric wards and operating theatres to endoscopy suites and international hospitals, now channelled into exceptional home-based care.

Registered Nurse, Nursing Council of Kenya (NCK)
Paediatric Clinical Nurse, Military Medical City Qatar
OR and PACU Nurse, 3rd Park Hospital Nairobi
Endoscopy Nurse, Makueni Doctors Plaza
Diploma in Nursing, Kenya Medical Training College
Our leadership

The team behind Zuri

Two visionary leaders united by one purpose — bringing professional, compassionate home care to every Kenyan family that needs it.

Kamene John
Co-Founder & Operations Lead

Kamene John is the co-founder of Zuri Home Care Services, bringing strategic vision and operational excellence to the company. With a deep commitment to expanding quality healthcare access across Kenya, Faith plays a pivotal role in shaping the organisation's direction, client relationships, and community outreach.

Passionate about bridging the gap between clinical care and compassionate service delivery, Faith works hand-in-hand with our clinical team to ensure every patient and family receives not just treatment, but a truly supportive care experience.

Connect on LinkedIn
"Our mission is simple: every Kenyan family deserves access to high-quality, compassionate healthcare, right in their own home. We built Zuri to make that a reality."
Kamene John, Co-Founder
Strategic Operations & Business Development
Client Relations & Community Partnerships
Healthcare Access Advocacy
Evans Kioko
Co-Founder & Business Development Lead

Evans Kioko brings entrepreneurial energy and strategic insight to Zuri Home Care Services. With a passion for building sustainable healthcare solutions across Kenya, Evans drives the company's growth, partnerships, and expansion into new communities.

Evans believes that quality home care shouldn't be a privilege — it should be accessible to every Kenyan family. His focus on building trust, scaling operations, and forging meaningful partnerships is what positions Zuri as Kenya's most reliable home care provider.

"We started Zuri because every Kenyan family deserves a trusted care partner — someone who shows up with expertise, kindness, and genuine commitment. That's what we're building."
Evans Kioko, Co-Founder

How it works

Getting started is
simple

01
Get in touch
Call, WhatsApp, or fill in the enquiry form. Tell us about the patient's needs and location.
02
Free home assessment
We visit your home, assess the patient, and discuss goals, preferences, and care requirements.
03
Personalised care plan
A customised care plan is prepared and shared with you and the patient's doctor for alignment.
04
Care begins
Your dedicated nurse begins visits on the agreed schedule. Regular updates keep you fully informed.

all conditions

Join our team

Grow your career in
home-based care

We are always looking for dedicated, compassionate healthcare professionals to join the Zuri family. If you are committed to delivering exceptional patient care, we want to hear from you.

Registered Nurse (RN)
NCK-registered nurses with experience in home care, post-surgical, or general nursing. Full-time and part-time roles available.
Certified Nursing Assistant (CNA)
Trained CNAs to support nursing staff with personal care, mobility assistance, and daily patient activities in the home setting.
Physiotherapist
Qualified physiotherapists to deliver rehabilitation and mobility programmes for post-surgical, stroke, and elderly patients at home.
Other Healthcare Professionals
Nutritionists, social workers, medical officers, and other allied health professionals passionate about community-based care.
Apply now

Send us your details and we'll be in touch. All applications are treated with the strictest confidentiality. We welcome both experienced professionals and recent graduates eager to grow.

Competitive, fair compensation
Flexible scheduling options
Supportive, professional team environment
Continuous professional development

Common questions

Everything you need
to know

We know choosing a home care provider is a significant decision. Here are answers to the questions families most commonly ask us.

If you don't find your answer here, our team is available by phone, WhatsApp, or email — and always happy to help.

Still have questions?
WhatsApp Us Now +254 701 822 465
Yes. All our nurses are fully registered with the Nursing Council of Kenya (NCK) and undergo thorough background checks before joining our team. We also verify qualifications and professional references. You will never receive a nurse we haven't personally vetted.
In most cases, we can arrange a free home assessment within 24–48 hours and begin care shortly after. For urgent situations — such as a patient being discharged from hospital — we prioritise same-day or next-day placement. Call or WhatsApp us immediately and we will do everything we can.
We primarily serve Nairobi, Kiambu County, Ruiru, Thika, and surrounding areas. We also offer telehealth consultations for diaspora families managing care of loved ones remotely. If you're outside our current coverage area, contact us — we may still be able to help.
During the free assessment, one of our clinical coordinators visits your home to meet the patient, review their medical history, assess their care needs, and understand any specific preferences. This allows us to create a fully personalised care plan and match the right nurse to your loved one — at no cost or obligation.
Our pricing is tailored to the specific services required, the frequency of visits, and the level of clinical care needed. We believe in complete transparency — no hidden fees. Following your free home assessment, we provide a clear, itemised quote. We accept M-Pesa, bank transfers, and cash, and can discuss flexible payment options for ongoing care.
Yes. We have experience with a wide range of complex conditions including dementia, Alzheimer's, Parkinson's, cancer, stroke recovery, HIV/AIDS management, and palliative care. Our nurses work closely with the patient's specialist doctors to ensure care aligns with their overall treatment plan.
Absolutely. Many of our clients are diaspora families managing the care of parents or relatives in Kenya from abroad. We offer virtual consultations, regular digital updates, and remote monitoring coordination so you remain fully informed and in control — wherever you are in the world.
